item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm downvoting this for a few reasons. This kinda just feels like an edgy, overpowered murder-monster imo. Names like "Daughter of Shadows" or "Daughter of Darkness" really doesn't work for me. Plus, this article is lacking in clinical tone to the point where I believe it should be rewritten. I see the potential of an upvote from me, but with the way it currently is, it just wouldn't feel right.

item.title: Interesting Concept, but lacking in clinical tone
item.content:encoded: This feels a bit more informal than a normal foundation document, if it had the language cleaned up a bit, and some more addenda, I might upvote it. Until then it's a downvote for me I'm afraid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Top 1 question the SCP foundation can't answer.
1. Is Dr Bright gay?

Only joking, I'm the gay one here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've put them on many of my own articles.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Uh… Why is the lgbtq+ SCP logo on this SCP only? Is this an anomaly breach?

item.title: Too personal
item.content:encoded: The last two paragraphs need to be re-written to be impersonal. This isn't supposed to be a personal report

item.title: Her name.
item.content:encoded:
छाया की बेटी
chhaaya kee betee

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Excellently written and original, but feels like it could be expanded on.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Really hasn't aged well.

item.title: Kinda reminds me of this little ditty.....
item.content:encoded:
The only God has broken the chain of golden age
When gods and every human where connected by the chain
It's time for re-enchantment, to call the gods again
To worship Mother Kali and to make this aeon end

Though really, this one is one of the greats. Whoever got that picture deserves a medal.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This kinda just feels like a cult leader with some crazy assassin skills. Not a lot of it screams 'SCP' you know. The few 'powers' she has seem a little tacked on. Also, the dictation of the containment procedures feels so off. WAY too many uses of "to be".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Reading through series I numerically, tis is the first amazing photo on the site!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I posted about this years ago myself, I said something along the lines "I see we like the same books". I think it's since been changed at least a little, because it isn't clearly a copy of Booboo anymore. I think it used to include the cage that Goblin and One-Eye made for her too.

I think I remember it used to have a lot more dead giveaways. Now all it has is the mind control and strangler cult stuff. Did Booboo have the black skin?

Edit: honestly I may just be confusing the book and the old writing of this, I'm probably being way too reactive.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought it also implies that Light was questioning if the researcher had fallen to her spell to some extent.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm downvoting and really think this needs a rewrite bad. I know the author already came clean about this, but it really is just way too close to a character in a previous work. The only substantial changes to the character are the black/white skin and being in the modern world instead of a medieval fantasy one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The denial of her requests seems to be nothing but unnecessary cruelty. The risk in giving her objects is that she can use them as weapons, yes? And ostensibly, she shouldn’t have anything within her cell to use the weapons against, so why would basic human amenities be denied? Surely the Foundation can find a way to transport a bed into a cell without having a couple of dudes just carry it in.

Maybe if there was some sort of incident report, early in her containment, that described how she used a bed or bookcase or whatever to breach containment somehow, and now is no longer allowed such amenities.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Just a small wording suggestion: Using the phrase "screaming at the top of her lungs" seemed a bit out of place to me in a Foundation document. This may be a personal preference but I figured I'd mention it.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
You certainly have a point, but the Foundation can actively communicate with 2820 and this anomaly is an ideal target for its ideology. I could certainly see them informing it about 029 as an attempt to induce its targeting. 2820's listed notable targets were reality benders, but its stated that it has targeted many individuals who were anomalous "in some way" suggesting variety.

As for complications and efficiency, I find that's far outweighed by the thematic and religious connection. Besides, the tone of this article lends itself to that sort of ad hoc solution.

item.title: Re: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
I'd have to disagree with tbis proposal because 2820 isn't something they've actually got control of. The targets are randomly selected, so it's not something they can use to target a specific anomaly, and even if they could, I'm not entirely certain it would make sense to use given its method of operation being a randomized series of events and the potential for unforseen complications in its (heh) execution.

Plus, I think 2820 primarilly targets reality benders anyways.

item.title: Wikiwalk Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
edit: edit made

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please pm a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Show
- Hide

To link to another article inspired by Indian mythology, I propose the following edit:

Also since said event, SCP-029 has redoubled her efforts to escape.

to

Also since said event, SCP-029 has redoubled her efforts to escape. SCP-2820 has been proposed as a possible method of neutralization should the situation worsen.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yes, this right here, was meant to be implied.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I am suprised that SCP-029 has her own game on steam.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The picture's caption says it was taken before incident 029-53b.

"As of incident 029-53b, anyone who has potential access to SCP-029 is required to first watch Surveillance Tape 029-Bodeen…"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oops, sorry Duck or I didn't reply to this earlier!

IRL, I was wearing clothes because Wikidot actually has nudity rules regarding images, and even chest-up without the breasts visible was something I considered too risky (and also just awkward, let's be real); in-universe, on the other hand, it's assumed that 029 is wearing clothes because the picture was taken after these notes were added (as per the caption's time frame reference):

This is ridiculous! The girl can't even have clothes? We are not animals, let her cover herself! -Dr. Ericka Bodeen

Dr. Bodeen, you are granted permission to deliver clothes to SCP-029. -Dr. Light

(At least, that's my understanding. All I did was cosplay, Duck wrote the actual article, so ultimately it's up to him.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm kind of confused. In the picture, SCP-029 is wearing clothes. However, the article makes it a point to state that 029 has been denied clothes, and does not wear any. So, which one is it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
See, I sort of came to the conclusion that the aforementioned researcher had been bugging the shit out of Light about the scip. "No it can't have clothes." "NO, it can not have clothes." "Fine, you want it to have clothes? Request granted, go take it some."

Light may have been chided for the action after, but there'd come a point when your research staff has been badgering you for so long (possibly even years) that their inherent usefulness would come into question. Who'd want a researcher that won't listen, especially when repeatedly denied? Sounds like a risk to keep on staff, and if there's one thing I know of the Foundation, they don't like having to babysit their staff and the anomalies. At least, not any more.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Seems like a Mary Sue. How did this get so many votes?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow, the addition of the picture is a really nice touch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I gotta say I agree with this, it would make a lot more sense to authorize sending a D-Class to give it the clothes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: S'all good!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Ultimately it's up to Duckman, but the new version looks fine to me. I sent him a lower resolution on Tumblr, though, just in case.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Finally found out how to make thumbnails with this new image block. Added one myself, feel free to revert if you don't like it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't know, that's the only part of the article I actually do like (aside from the picture now).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Aw yeah, Cyan, you actually did the 029 cosplay!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Nice picture. But now that I've actually read this, I have to downvote because sending a knowledgeable, valuable researcher to their [DATA EXPUNGED] in order to make a point is fucking stupid.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
S'pretty damn epic if you ask me. And Keter-class at that.

Also, to GeneR down there regarding the image: After I Photoshopped it I posted it on Tumblr, and then Ducky got it from there, but I was expecting he'd size it down/alter it a little before posting. I'll send him a smaller size to use.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great job!

Care to make a thumbnail though? The picture is 1.9 megabytes big and takes quite some time to load.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: A number of Gears' articles are his kids…

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Are there any other SCP whose picture is of an actual member of the site? This is really freakin' neat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow. How does it feel for you to be the literal face of an SCP?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dat pic tho :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm the one who is honorer… all I did was write the bloody thing, you put time and effort into make up and pictures!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yep, it is me! :D This is such an honor, you know, especially since it's a Series I article.

Edit: Heh, it didn't take too much effort. I doubt as much as writing it did. An hour and fifteen or so for the body paint, ten minutes in the stairwell for the pictures (Palmetto28 took those, by the way), and twenty minutes in Photoshop for the black eyes and lighting effects. Altogether it was only about three hours, counting the extremely thorough shower afterward. I'm still stained gray even though I washed four times. :P

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Completely agree here. It's a really nice touch.

Btw, I think the user who's name you're thinking of is Cyantreuse.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, this article now has a picture! I'd like to give a really big thank you to Cyan, whose site user name I can not remember because I am horrible like that, but he did an AMAZING job making himself up to look like SCP-029. I am just… in awe. I think it adds a lot to the article!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
When you say "can turn any item into a weapon"are we talkin like Jackie Chan style or straight up murder your ass with belly button lint?

Would love to see her throw down with Abel.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is easily my least favorite Keter, to me she's just an X-Woman who can kill people with a pair of pants and mind control men. The Kali bit does nothing for me.
Why can't the Foundation simply sedate, gas or even drown her?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Imagining her and 173 strangling each other. Good times.

Mythology based SCPs may be done a little too often, but eyyy, I enjoyed this, especially the imagery involved. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
In all my years, I've somehow managed to skip over this double-digit SCP… From a 2015 perspective, I'm not impressed. The premise "woman who is really black and really evil" needs some SERIOUSLY good writing to pull off, and this just isn't it. I'm also not a fan of some of the structural elements, including the back-and-forth communication in the containment procedures.

That said, I suppose the chances of a rewrite happening on an SCP with a score of +150 isn't huge, so I'm just going to -1 and move on for now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"This is ridiculous! The girl can't even have clothes? We are not animals, let her cover herself! -Dr. Ericka Bodeen
Dr. Bodeen, you are granted permission to deliver clothes to SCP-029 -Dr. Light"

I'm not a fan of conversations in a special containment procedures. But I like the fact that she refers herself as "Daughter of Shadows/Night/Darkness."

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought so when I first read it. It isn't like Cook invented Kali though, or the Hindu concept of Avatar. You just read his work and then drank from the same well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: So, I was recently re-reading the Black Company books by Glen Cook and realized that I'd taken some ideas for this scp with out thinking of it, largely in the naming structure. Daughter of Darkness, Kali Yuga, both seems to come from that. I feel like the content is different from the actual character, but felt the need to note that here.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Doctor Catapult, you are given permission to bleach her skin."

Edit: Ok, I got nothing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Constant attempts to escape, endlessly tries to kill people, and the potentially catastrophic anomaly released when she is all black can be released without direct action on her part even when she is contained.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why is this keter? Box test places it at Euclid at most.

item.title: Re: Preparing for hate
item.content:encoded: Thanks for not stabbing me

item.title: Re: Preparing for hate
item.content:encoded:
"What I'm doing is wrong, I know it's wrong, but I'm going to do it anyway."

Seriously, why bring it up if you know it's only going to give you eye-rolls? You know that the answer is always going to be: "682 lives through X, and kills people shortly afterwards."

It has plot armor. It can't die because the plot demands it. That's the point of the skip.

item.title: Preparing for hate
item.content:encoded:
I do not want to sound like a new person [well, I am….], but I really wanted to know………

can we test it on the hard to destroy reptile??

Please don't stab me. I have cookies.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'd love to see a tale written about this and SCP-1790 being related through their Thuggee roots.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There are a multitude of shades of black.
